message:
perf(channel): migrate oneshot, clock_skew, crash, and fault channels from std Mutex to parking_lot

Complete the channel subsystem's parking_lot migration (broadcast, mpsc,
watch, and partition were migrated in earlier commits).

oneshot: Replace all std::sync::Mutex usage with parking_lot::Mutex,
removing 12 .expect("oneshot lock poisoned") calls and 4 if-let-Ok/Err
bail-on-poison patterns in Drop impls (Sender, SendPermit, Receiver,
RecvFuture). The poison-bail pattern in Drop was a defensive measure
against double-panic abort — parking_lot's non-poisoning semantics
make this unnecessary. Also flatten multi-line .lock().expect() chains
into single-line .lock() calls in is_closed(), is_ready(), etc.
Update all test assertions from .lock().unwrap() to .lock().

clock_skew: Migrate SkewClock RNG guard from std Mutex, removing
.expect("skew rng lock poisoned").

crash: Migrate CrashController state and RNG from std Mutex, removing
4 .expect("crash state lock poisoned") and 1 .expect("crash rng lock
poisoned"). Flatten multi-line lock chains for is_crashed(),
is_exhausted(), restart_mode().

fault: Migrate FaultSender RNG and reorder_buffer from std Mutex,
removing 5 .expect("fault rng lock poisoned"/"reorder buffer poisoned")
calls. Simplify buffered_count() from .lock().map_or(0, ..) to
.lock().len(). Remove .expect() in test capacity assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
